title: solanum agrimoniifolium
intro: Solanum agrimoniifolium, also known as the Japanese potato or sweet shrub, is a species of flowering plant in the Solanaceae family. The leaves and roots of the plant have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Japan and Korea, where they are valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Solanum agrimoniifolium include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Solanum agrimoniifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as powders and salves.
